english: Seven falls, eight struggles
meaning: Going through great pain and struggle

english: The moon and a turtle
meaning: Vast difference; not comparable

english: Good medicine tastes bitter
meaning: Helpful advice can be hard to hear

english: A cat's forehead
meaning: Very small space

english: Carelessness is the greatest enemy
meaning: Don't let your guard down

english: Stomach 80 percent full
meaning: Eat in moderation

english: what goes around comes around
meaning: You reap what you sow

japanese: 喉元過ぎれば熱さを忘れる
english: Once it passes your throat, you forget the heat
meaning: People forget hardships once they are over

japanese: 二兎を追う者は一兎をも得ず
romaji: Ni to wo ou mono wa itto wo mo ezu
english: One who chases two rabbits catches neither
meaning: Focus on one thing at a time

romaji: Senri no michi mo ippo kara
english: A journey of a thousand miles begins with one step
meaning: Start small

romaji: Hari no ana kara ten wo nozoku
english: Peek at the sky through a needle's eye
meaning: A narrow viewpoint leads to poor judgment

romaji: Nen ni wa nen wo ireyo
english: Put care into care
meaning: Double-check; be extra careful

english: Hide your head, but not your butt
meaning: Trying to hide something obvious

english: Even monkeys fall from trees
meaning: Experts also make mistakes

english: With comfort comes hardship
meaning: Life has ups and downs

japanese: 縁は異なもの味なもの
romaji: En wa inamono aji na mono
english: Fate is strange yet delightful
meaning: Unexpected connections can be good

english: After rain, the ground hardens
meaning: Difficult situations strengthen relationships or foundations

japanese: 犬も歩けば棒に当たる
romaji: Inu mo arukeba bou ni ataru
english: Even a walking dog will hit a stick
meaning: Try anything and you might get lucky (or unlucky)

english: It's darkest under the lighthouse
meaning: The obvious is often overlooked

english: Good medicine tastes bitter
meaning: Helpful advice often sounds harsh

english: After ease comes hardship
meaning: Good times and bad times alternate

japanese: 三人寄れば文殊の知恵
english: Three people together have the wisdom of Monju
meaning: Two heads are better than one

romaji: Warau kado ni wa fuku kitaru
english: Fortune comes to a laughing gate
meaning: Happiness and good fortune come to those who smile

english: Tap a stone bridge before crossing
meaning: Be very cautious

english: Hurry to do good
meaning: If it's worth doing, do it at once

japanese: 藪をつついて蛇を出す
romaji: Yabu wo tsutsuite hebi wo dasu
english: Poke the bush and bring out a snake
meaning: Stir up trouble unnecessarily

romaji: Warau kado ni wa fuku kitaru
english: Fortune comes to a laughing gate
meaning: Good fortune comes to those who smile

japanese: 明日は明日の風が吹く
romaji: Ashita wa ashita no kaze ga fuku
english: Tomorrow's wind will blow tomorrow
meaning: Don't worry too much about the future or things you cannot control right now. Tomorrow will bring its own solutions and circumstances.

english: Not speaking is the flower
meaning: Some things are better left unsaid. Silence can be more beautiful, effective, or sophisticated than over-explaining or stating the obvious.

english: Continuance is power
meaning: Consistency is the key to mastery. Even small efforts, when repeated daily, accumulate into a formidable strength that cannot be easily broken.

japanese: 聞くは一時の恥、聞かぬは一生の恥
romaji: Kiku wa isshonoji no haji, kikanu wa isshō no haji
english: To ask is a moment's shame; not to ask is a lifetime's shame
meaning: It is better to admit ignorance and learn something new than to pretend to know and remain ignorant forever.

romaji: Ki wo mite mori wo mizu
english: See the trees but not the forest
meaning: Missing the bigger picture

english: Water on a hot stone
meaning: A futile effort with little effect

japanese: 井の中の蛙大海を知らず
romaji: I no naka no kawazu taikai wo shirazu
english: A frog in a well doesn't know the ocean
meaning: Limited experience limits perspective

romaji: Tabi wa michizure yo wa nasake
english: Travel needs companions; life needs kindness
meaning: Relationships and compassion matter

japanese: 為せば成る為さねば成らぬ何事も
romaji: Naseba naru nasaneba naranu nanigoto mo
english: If you try, you can do it; if you don’t try, you cannot do anything
meaning: Success depends entirely on effort. Without action, nothing is possible.

japanese: 出る杭は打たれるが、出過ぎた杭は打たれない
romaji: Deru kui wa utareru ga, desugita kui wa utarenai
english: The nail that sticks out gets hammered down, but the one that sticks out too much does not
meaning: Moderate deviation invites criticism, but exceptional excellence can transcend opposition.

english: Unskilled but enthusiastic
meaning: Someone may lack skill but still deeply enjoy what they do. Passion does not require mastery.

english: Not knowing is Buddha
meaning: Ignorance can sometimes bring peace of mind. Knowing everything is not always beneficial.

english: If you live there, it becomes the capital
meaning: Any place can become comfortable and meaningful once you get used to it.

romaji: Nasake wa hito no tame narazu
english: Kindness is not just for others
meaning: Helping others ultimately benefits yourself as well.

romaji: Koubou ni mo fude no ayamari
english: Even Kobo makes mistakes with his brush
meaning: Even experts make mistakes sometimes.

english: Eye drops from the second floor
meaning: An ineffective or impractical method of doing something.

romaji: Wataru seken ni oni wa nai
english: There are no demons in the world you walk through
meaning: Most people are kinder than you expect.

japanese: 鳴かぬなら鳴かせてみせようホトトギス
english: If it won’t sing, I’ll make it sing
meaning: Represents a proactive, strategic approach to solving problems (associated with leadership style).

japanese: 鳴かぬなら鳴くまで待とうホトトギス
romaji: Nakanu nara naku made matou hototogisu
english: If it won’t sing, wait until it does
meaning: Represents patience and timing as a strategy.

japanese: 鳴かぬなら殺してしまえホトトギス
english: If it won’t sing, kill it
meaning: Represents a ruthless, decisive leadership approach.

japanese: 鶏口となるも牛後となるなかれ
romaji: Keikou to naru mo gyuugo to naru nakare
english: Better be the head of a chicken than the tail of a cow
meaning: It is better to lead a small group than to follow in a large one.

romaji: Fune ni kizami te ken wo motomu
english: Carving a mark on the boat to find a lost sword
meaning: Using outdated methods for changing situations leads to failure.

japanese: 朱に交われば赤くなる
english: If you mix with vermilion, you become red
meaning: People are influenced by their environment and associations.

japanese: 青は藍より出でて藍より青し
romaji: Ao wa ai yori idete ai yori aoshi
english: Blue comes from indigo but is bluer than indigo
meaning: Students can surpass their teachers.

romaji: Ki wo mite mori wo mizu
english: See the trees but not the forest
meaning: Miss the bigger picture

english: Even a packhorse driver looks good in fine clothes
meaning: Clothes make the person

english: Dumplings over flowers
meaning: Practicality over aesthetics; choosing substance over appearance

english: Time is money
meaning: Time is valuable and should not be wasted

english: If you're in a hurry, take a detour
meaning: The safest or most reliable way is often not the quickest

english: If you eat poison, eat the plate too
meaning: If you must do it, go all the way

english: Do laundry while the ogre is away
meaning: Take advantage of someone's absence

english: The nail that sticks out gets hammered down
meaning: Standing out invites criticism

english: Tap a stone bridge before crossing
meaning: Be very cautious

english: Just watching is a pleasure
meaning: Enjoy observing; you don't always need to participate

english: Fall seven times, get up eight
meaning: Never give up

japanese: 二度あることは三度ある
romaji: Nido aru koto wa sando aru
english: What happens twice will happen a third time
meaning: Patterns tend to repeat

english: Not knowing is Buddha
meaning: Ignorance is bliss

romaji: Ishi no ue ni mo sannen
english: Three years on a stone
meaning: Perseverance leads to success; even a cold stone warms up if you sit on it long enough

english: Even monkeys fall from trees
meaning: Even experts make mistakes

english: A bee on a crying face
meaning: Misfortune piles up

japanese: 二度あることは三度ある
romaji: Nido aru koto wa sando aru
english: What happens twice will happen a third time
meaning: Patterns tend to repeat

english: Failure is the origin of success
meaning: Mistakes lead to growth

japanese: 可愛い子には旅をさせよ
romaji: Kawaii ko ni wa tabi wo saseyo
english: Send the beloved child on a journey
meaning: If you love your children, let them face challenges and see the world rather than overprotecting them.

english: Counting the skins of tanuki you haven't caught
meaning: Don't count your chickens before they hatch; don't plan based on profits that aren't guaranteed yet.

english: Gold coins to a cat
meaning: Giving something of high value to someone who cannot appreciate or understand its worth.

english: Spilled water does not return to the tray
meaning: It is no use crying over spilled milk; once something is done, it cannot be undone.

english: One time, one meeting
meaning: Treasure every encounter, for it may never happen again; once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.

english: Early rising is worth three coins
meaning: Getting up early brings benefits

english: Praising one's own painting
meaning: Blowing your own trumpet; self-praise.

romaji: Waza wai wo tenjite fuku to nasu
english: To turn a misfortune into a blessing
meaning: Making the best of a bad situation or learning from a disaster to create success.

english: Never forget your beginner's spirit
meaning: Always remember the humility and enthusiasm you had when you first started something.

japanese: 井の中の蛙大海を知らず
romaji: I no naka no kawazu taikai wo shirazu
english: A frog in a well does not know the great sea
meaning: Someone who sees the world through a narrow perspective because they haven't traveled or explored.

japanese: 郷に入っては郷に従え
romaji: Gou ni itte wa gou ni shitagae
english: When you enter a village, follow the village
meaning: When in Rome, do as the Romans do; adapt to local customs.

english: If in a hurry, go around
meaning: The safer path can be faster

japanese: 井の中の蛙大海を知らず
romaji: I no naka no kawazu taikai wo shirazu
english: A frog in a well does not know the great sea
meaning: Limited experience limits perspective

english: Regret does not stand before
meaning: It's too late for regrets

english: Fame from a mistake
meaning: A lucky break that comes from an error

english: The day you decide is a lucky day
meaning: Start now

english: Not knowing is Buddha
meaning: Ignorance is bliss

english: Spilled water will not return to the tray
meaning: What is done cannot be undone

english: A single thought pierces even rock
meaning: With unwavering determination and focus, even impossible tasks can be achieved

japanese: 二兎を追う者は一兎をも得ず
romaji: Nito o ou mono wa ittoto mo ezu
english: He who chases two rabbits catches neither
meaning: If you try to do two things at once, you may end up succeeding at neither

romaji: Nito o ou mono wa ittoto mo ezu
english: He who chases two rabbits catches neither
meaning: If you try to do two things at once, you may end up succeeding at neither

english: A bee on a crying face
meaning: Misfortune never comes alone

english: Even monkeys fall from trees
meaning: Experts can make mistakes

english: If fish has heart, water has heart
meaning: Good intentions are reciprocated

english: The nail that sticks out gets hammered down
meaning: Standing out invites criticism

english: Good causes bring good results
meaning: Good deeds lead to good outcomes

english: Kindness is not for others alone
meaning: Kindness comes back to you

english: See the trees but not the forest
meaning: Missing the bigger picture
